channel.title: Comments for page "Black Autumn II: Rise Of the Pit Sloth Hub"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Black Autumn II: Rise Of the Pit Sloth Hub"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thoroughly enjoyed reading Black Autumn I last October, and I'm thoroughly enjoying this one right now. Stumbling across SCP-4040 is what introduced me to the S&C Plastics canon in the first place, which is by far my favorite part of the site.

In case there's anyone else late to the party like me, and like atmospheric music while reading, I found this youtube playlist someone, I presume a site member, made. Been working out pretty well for me:
https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLhz0tYINcv2kQkYmvmXauNTY6oQBpeQBD

item.title: I see what you're doing
item.content:encoded:
Director Weiss

Agents Blake and Ruby

I know a RWBY fan when I see one.

item.title: Re: ♪Let's all go to the lobby, let's all go to the lobby♪
item.content:encoded: I love how the hub itself is a tale of its own which gives a second prospective on the events at the end of the narrative arc!

item.title: Re: ♪Let's all go to the lobby, let's all go to the lobby♪
item.content:encoded: Just piggybacking (goatmanbacking?) your comment to say that I am absolutely loving this series so far, and can't wait to keep reading more! I like it even more than the first Black Autumn hub, which was phenomenal on its own. Please keep writing; I'm addicted!

item.title: Tremble at my abhorrent misuse of gouache paint!
item.content:encoded: goddamit ihp, you're too sweet. I can't thank you enough for this.

item.title: Re: ♪Let's all go to the lobby, let's all go to the lobby♪
item.content:encoded:
As much as I love this series, I’m going to have to downvote due to the fact that you’ve gotten the “Let’s all go to the lobby” song stuck in my head.

You monster.

item.title: ♪Let's all go to the lobby, let's all go to the lobby♪
item.content:encoded:
Firstly: I have to give so much thanks to Mendelssohn for doing the poster for this. You friggin' rock.

Like I said in the author commentary for Burning Screen, the conceit of this series was intended to be that the reader was viewing a movie, and that the tales were to be named after scenes from it. Only "Opening Credits" (and possibly another tale later on) are going to follow that conceit, but I think that it works well enough for the hub, and I couldn't not use the amazing poster they made.

I'm not quite as enthused with this as I am with my homage to the Buzzing from the Black Autumn Hub, but I like it all the same.

Hope you enjoy reading this as it develops.
